page: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this server operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, ensuring zero resource sharing at the CPU level. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8g.metal-24xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 163,903.0 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 26,474.0. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidths, reaching 481.1 GB/sec for large-block reads and 201.25 GB/sec for large-block writes. The server also excels in database operations, processing 196.18 million Redis SET operations per second, and static web serving, where it delivers a throughput of 102.43 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier, and text generation for very small LLM models is relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal Graviton architecture offers high resource density and strong multi-threaded performance per unit of power, making it highly efficient for scaled compute workloads. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network storage solutions, and the lack of x86 instruction sets such as AVX or AMX. The instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, it achieves top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside exceptional throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core execution and small-model LLM text generation are less competitive. Given its high multi-threaded efficiency and bare-metal resource density, the c8g.metal-24xl is highly suited for software compilation, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized compute workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized arm64 server with 96 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, lacking local storage.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized arm64 server delivering high-density multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores on arm64 architecture
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded compute
